# RP2040 / RP2350 PIO Assembly Cheat Sheet Generated by Grok 4.5

**9 instructions · 16-bit opcodes · 1 cycle each + optional delay**  
Reference: RP2040 datasheet §3.4 · RP2350 PIO is a superset (notes at end)

---

## Architecture (one State Machine)

```
                ┌──────────┐   autopull    ┌─────┐  OUT   pins / X / Y / …
   TX FIFO ───► │   OSR    │ ───────────►  │ SM  │ ─────────────────────►
   (4×32)       │ shift out│               │     │
                └──────────┘               │     │  IN    pins / X / Y / …
                ┌──────────┐   autopush    │     │ ◄─────────────────────
   RX FIFO ◄─── │   ISR    │ ◄───────────  │     │
   (4×32)       │ shift in │               └─────┘
                └──────────┘                  │
           scratch X, Y  ◄────────────────────┘
           PC, delay counter, shift counters
```

| Resource | Size | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Instruction RAM | 32 words / PIO block | Shared by 4 SMs |
| PC | 5-bit | Wraps via EXECCTRL wrap_top/bottom |
| OSR / ISR | 32-bit | Output / Input Shift Registers |
| X, Y | 32-bit | Scratch; counters via `jmp x--` / `y--` |
| TX/RX FIFO | 4×32 each | Join → one 8-deep FIFO |
| Side-set | 0–5 bits | Parallel pin write with any instr |
| Delay | 0–31 cycles | Shares field with side-set |
| IRQ flags | 8 per PIO | SM-visible; lower 4 → NVIC |

**RP2040:** 2 PIO × 4 SM · **RP2350:** 3 PIO × 4 SM (+ extras below)

---

## Instruction encoding (every instr is 16 bits)

```
15        13 12          8 7                     0
┌───────────┬─────────────┬───────────────────────┐
│  opcode   │ delay/side  │     instruction data  │
└───────────┴─────────────┴───────────────────────┘
```

- **Every instruction takes 1 cycle**, then optional **delay** cycles (PC held).
- Side-set bits (if any) are applied **at the start** of the instruction.
- Delay field width = `5 − SIDESET_COUNT` (or `4 − side` if `.side_set N opt`).

| Opcode | Mnemonic | Purpose |
|:---:|---|---|
| 000 | `JMP` | Conditional / unconditional branch |
| 001 | `WAIT` | Stall until condition |
| 010 | `IN` | Shift bits into ISR |
| 011 | `OUT` | Shift bits out of OSR |
| 100 | `PUSH` / `PULL` | FIFO ↔ shift register (encoded as one op) |
| 101 | `MOV` | Copy / bit-reverse / invert between regs |
| 110 | `IRQ` | Set / clear / wait IRQ flags |
| 111 | `SET` | Immediate write (small constant) |

Pseudo-ops: `NOP` = `mov y, y` · `PUSH`/`PULL` share opcode 100.

---

## All 9 instructions

### JMP — branch
```
jmp             <label>          ; always
jmp !x          <label>          ; if X == 0
jmp x--         <label>          ; if X != 0, then X = X - 1
jmp !y          <label>          ; if Y == 0
jmp y--         <label>          ; if Y != 0, then Y = Y - 1
jmp x!=y        <label>          ; if X != Y
jmp pin         <label>          ; if JMP_PIN GPIO is high
jmp !osre       <label>          ; if OSR not empty (shift count < pull_thresh)
```
- Condition checked **before** decrement on `x--`/`y--`.
- Fall-through if false (still 1 cycle).
- `jmp pin` uses absolute GPIO from EXECCTRL.JMP_PIN (not mapped by `in_base`).

### WAIT — stall until condition
```
wait <pol> gpio  <gpio>          ; absolute GPIO number
wait <pol> pin   <idx>           ; pin relative to in_base
wait <pol> irq   <irq> [(rel)]   ; IRQ flag (rel = SM-relative index)
```
- `pol` = `0` wait for low, `1` wait for high.
- Stalls (0 progress) until true; side-set still applies each stalled cycle…  
  **No:** side-set applies when the instruction **first begins**; while stalled, pins hold. Delay runs after condition met.

### IN — shift into ISR
```
in <source>, <bitcount>          ; bitcount 1..32  (32 encoded as 0)
```
| Source | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `pins` | GPIOs from `in_base` |
| `x` / `y` | Scratch |
| `null` | Zeros (shift in 0s — useful to flush) |
| `isr` | ISR ← ISR (rotate/realign) |
| `osr` | Sample OSR into ISR |

- Shift direction: `IN_SHIFTDIR` (0=left / MSB first into ISR, 1=right).
- After `PUSH_THRESH` bits: **autopush** if enabled → RX FIFO, ISR cleared.
- Does **not** clear ISR otherwise.

### OUT — shift out of OSR
```
out <dest>, <bitcount>           ; bitcount 1..32
```
| Dest | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `pins` | Write `out_base` .. `out_base+OUT_COUNT-1` |
| `x` / `y` | Load scratch (remaining OSR bits discarded per shift) |
| `null` | Discard bits |
| `pindirs` | Set pin directions |
| `pc` | Write PC (jump via data!) |
| `isr` | OSR bits → ISR |
| `exec` | Execute the shifted bits as an instruction |

- Shift direction: `OUT_SHIFTDIR` (0=left, 1=right).
- After `PULL_THRESH` bits emptied: **autopull** if enabled.
- `OUT_STICKY`: last OUT/SET remains driven until next write.
- `INLINE_OUT_EN`: one data bit acts as write-enable.

### PUSH / PULL — FIFO transfer
```
push [iffull] [block|noblock]    ; ISR → RX FIFO; ISR := 0
pull [ifempty] [block|noblock]   ; TX FIFO → OSR
```
| Qualifier | Effect |
|---|---|
| (default) | Always push/pull; **block** if FIFO full/empty |
| `iffull` | Push only if shift count ≥ PUSH_THRESH |
| `ifempty` | Pull only if OSR empty (count ≥ PULL_THRESH) |
| `noblock` | Don't stall: push→drop if full; pull→OSR=X if empty |

- `pull` copies TX FIFO word into OSR (or X if noblock+empty).
- Autopush/autopull usually replace explicit push/pull in streaming code.

### MOV — copy / transform
```
mov <dest>, <src>
mov <dest>, ::<src>              ; bit-reverse
mov <dest>, ~<src>               ; bitwise invert
mov <dest>, ::~<src>             ; reverse then invert (or ~:: — same enc)
```
| Dest | Src |
|---|---|
| `pins`, `x`, `y`, `exec`, `pc`, `isr`, `osr` | `pins`, `x`, `y`, `null`, `status`, `isr`, `osr` |

- `mov exec, …` → execute the value as an instruction (1-shot).
- `mov pc, …` → jump.
- `status` = all-1s or all-0s from FIFO level compare (EXECCTRL.STATUS_*).
- `mov x, status` common for “TX space available?” tests.

### IRQ — flags
```
irq [set]      <irq> [(rel)]     ; set flag (default)
irq wait       <irq> [(rel)]     ; set, then stall until cleared by CPU/SM
irq clear      <irq> [(rel)]     ; clear flag
```
- 8 flags (0–7). `rel` adds SM index (mod 4) to irq number — SM-private slots.
- Flags 0–3 also wire to system IRQ lines.

### SET — immediate (5-bit data = 0..31)
```
set pins,    <val>               ; write set_base .. set_base+SET_COUNT-1
set pindirs, <val>               ; directions for those pins
set x,       <val>               ; X := val (zero-extended)
set y,       <val>
```
- Only 5-bit immediates — for full 32-bit loads use `pull` / `mov` / `out`.

### NOP
```
nop                              ; = mov y, y
nop side 1 [3]                   ; useful pure delay + side-set vehicle
```

---

## Side-set & delay (every instruction)

```
.side_set <N>            ; N bits of side-set on EVERY instruction (max delay 2^(5-N)-1)
.side_set <N> opt        ; optional side-set; enable bit steals 1; delay max 2^(4-N)-1
.side_set <N> pindirs    ; side-set drives directions, not values
```

```
<instr>  side <bits>  [<delay>]
out pins, 1  side 0  [3]         ; side-set at cycle-start; then instr; then 3 delay cyc
jmp x-- loop side 1              ; bare instrs HOLD previous side-set value
```

**Critical rules**
1. Side-set updates **only** on instructions with an explicit `side`.
2. Instructions **without** `side` **hold** the last side-set value for their full duration (incl. delay).
3. This is why bare `jmp` / `push` / `set x` after a high side often **stretches BCLK high**.
4. Side-set base pin = `SIDESET_BASE`; bits map to consecutive GPIOs.

| SIDESET_COUNT | Max delay (mandatory side) | Max delay (`opt`) |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 31 | — |
| 1 | 15 | 7 |
| 2 | 7 | 3 |
| 3 | 3 | 1 |
| 4 | 1 | 0 |
| 5 | 0 | — |

---

## Program directives (pioasm)

```
.program <name>                  ; start a program
.wrap_target                     ; wrap bottom (default: first instr)
.wrap                            ; wrap top   (default: after last instr)
.origin <N>                      ; force load address
.side_set <N> [opt] [pindirs]
.define <name> <val>             ; constant
.word <val>                      ; raw 16-bit instruction
.lang_opt …                      ; language options for codegen
```

**RP2350-only pioasm FIFO modes**
```
.fifo tx                         ; join as TX-only 8-deep
.fifo rx                         ; join as RX-only 8-deep
.fifo txrx                       ; both (default unjoined 4+4)
.fifo putget                     ; RP2350 put/get duplex FIFO mode
```

```
.wrap_target
loop:
    …
.wrap                            ; free jump wrap_top → wrap_target (0 cycles)
```

Jump vs wrap: `jmp` costs 1 cycle; `.wrap` is free PC reload.

---

## Pin mapping (configured in C, used by asm)

| Config field | Used by | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `OUT_BASE` + `OUT_COUNT` | `out pins`, `mov pins` | Data out bus |
| `SET_BASE` + `SET_COUNT` (≤5) | `set pins` | Framing / control (LRCLK, CS…) |
| `SIDESET_BASE` + count | `side N` | Clock lines (BCLK, SCK…) |
| `IN_BASE` | `in pins`, `wait pin` | Data in bus |
| `JMP_PIN` | `jmp pin` | Single branch condition GPIO |

**All bases are absolute GPIO numbers.**  
`in pins` / `out pins` map consecutive pins from base (mod 32).

Typical I2S master map:
```
sideset = BCLK
set     = LRCLK
out     = DOUT
in      = DIN
```

---

## Shift registers & FIFOs

| Control | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `OUT_SHIFTDIR=0` | Left (MSB first out) — standard SPI/I2S |
| `OUT_SHIFTDIR=1` | Right (LSB first out) |
| `IN_SHIFTDIR=0` | Left (MSB lands in ISR MSB) |
| `IN_SHIFTDIR=1` | Right |
| `PULL_THRESH` | Bits out of OSR before autopull / `pull ifempty` (0 ⇒ 32) |
| `PUSH_THRESH` | Bits into ISR before autopush / `push iffull` (0 ⇒ 32) |
| `AUTOPULL` | Auto `pull` when OSR emptied past thresh |
| `AUTOPUSH` | Auto `push` when ISR filled past thresh |
| `FJOIN_TX` | TX steals RX storage → 8-deep TX, no RX |
| `FJOIN_RX` | RX steals TX storage → 8-deep RX, no TX |

**Data path (full duplex streaming)**
```
DMA → TX FIFO → [autopull] → OSR → out pins, N
in pins, N → ISR → [autopush] → RX FIFO → DMA
```

---

## Timing model (count every cycle)

```
total cycles for instr = 1 + delay
```
- Side-set asserted for the **entire** 1+delay window.
- `WAIT` / blocking `PUSH`/`PULL` / FIFO-stall on autopush/pull: SM freezes; clocks (side-set) hold last value.
- `.wrap` = 0 extra cycles · `jmp` = 1 cycle whether taken or not.

**Bit-loop template (N cycles/bit, clock on side-set)**
```
set x, <count-2>            ; for count iterations of body + 1 explicit last
bit:
    out pins, 1 side 0 [A]  ; low phase  = 1+A
    in  pins, 1 side 1 [B]  ; high phase = 1+B  (+1 if bare jmp holds high)
    jmp x-- bit             ; often holds previous side!
```
→ Put `side` on the `jmp` when you need it to finish a phase cleanly.

**Frame boundary rule (I2S etc.)**  
Do **31 tight iterations + dedicated last-bit block** so `push`/`set LRCLK`/`set x` overhead lands on the last bit’s low phase — not as a multi-cycle glitch between frames.

---

## Common recipes

**Count loop (32 bits)**
```
set x, 31
loop:
    …                        ; body once per bit
    jmp x-- loop             ; runs 32×  (entry x=31 → 31 taken jumps + 1 fall)
```
Or `set x, 30` + separate last-bit block = 31 + 1 = 32.

**Autopush RX word**
```
; C: sm_config_set_in_shift(&c, false, true, 32);
.wrap_target
    in pins, 1
.wrap                        ; after 32 ins → RX FIFO automatically
```

**Blocking wait for edge**
```
wait 0 pin 0                 ; wait DIN low
wait 1 pin 0                 ; then high → rising edge
```

**IRQ handshake between SMs**
```
; SM0:
irq set 0 rel
; SM1:
wait 1 irq 0 rel
irq clear 0 rel
```

**Load 32-bit constant into X**
```
pull                         ; CPU/DMA pre-loaded TX FIFO
mov x, osr
```

**Bit-reverse (LSB-first wire → MSB-first word)**
```
mov isr, ::isr
push
```

---

## C config quick ref (pico-sdk)

```c
pio_sm_config c = program_get_default_config(offset);

sm_config_set_clkdiv(&c, sysclk / desired_sm_hz);

sm_config_set_out_pins(&c, out_base, out_count);
sm_config_set_set_pins(&c, set_base, set_count);
sm_config_set_in_pins(&c, in_base);
sm_config_set_sideset_pins(&c, sideset_base);
sm_config_set_jmp_pin(&c, jmp_gpio);

sm_config_set_out_shift(&c, shift_right, autopull, pull_thresh);
sm_config_set_in_shift (&c, shift_right, autopush, push_thresh);
sm_config_set_fifo_join(&c, PIO_FIFO_JOIN_NONE); // or _TX / _RX

// pindirs: true=output
pio_sm_set_consecutive_pindirs(pio, sm, out_base, out_count, true);
pio_sm_set_consecutive_pindirs(pio, sm, set_base, set_count, true);
pio_sm_set_consecutive_pindirs(pio, sm, sideset_base, sideset_count, true);
pio_sm_set_consecutive_pindirs(pio, sm, in_base, in_count, false);

pio_gpio_init(pio, pin);     // each pin used
pio_sm_init(pio, sm, offset, &c);
pio_sm_set_enabled(pio, sm, true);
```

**Clock**
```
f_sm = f_sys / (clkdiv_int + clkdiv_frac/256)
```
For protocols with C cycles/bit: `f_sm = C × bit_rate`.

---

## Pitfalls (the ones that actually bite)

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| BCLK stretches at LRCLK edge | Bare `push`/`set`/`jmp` holds side | Last-bit block; put `side` on those instrs |
| Asymmetric high/low | Bare `jmp x--` holds previous side | `jmp x-- loop side 0` (or matching phase) |
| First sample trash | OSR empty at start / no prefill | Prefill TX FIFO; discard 1st RX word |
| SM freezes | Blocking pull on empty / push on full | DMA pace, or `noblock`, or deeper join |
| Wrong bit order | Shift dir default is **right** | `shift_right=false` for MSB-first |
| `set x, 32` impossible | SET imm is 5-bit (0..31) | `set x, 0` + use thresh 0 (=32) or pull |
| Side-set not changing | Forgot `side N` on that instr | Explicit `side` every time you need a change |
| `jmp pin` ignores in_base | Uses absolute JMP_PIN | Set `sm_config_set_jmp_pin` |
| Wrap jumps mid-instr delay? | No — delay completes first | OK by design |

---

## RP2350 extras (vs RP2040)

| Feature | Notes |
|---|---|
| 3 PIO blocks | PIO0/1/2, still 4 SM each, 32 instr each |
| `mov rxfifo[y], isr` etc. | Get/put indexed FIFO access (PIO v1) |
| `.fifo putget` | Duplex FIFO mode for simultaneous TX+RX semantics |
| `WAIT GPIO` enhanced | Additional wait sources in PIO-v1 |
| IRQ DMA | Extra DREQ/IRQ routing |
| Instruction opcodes | Same 9 base mnemonics; check datasheet §PIO for new MOV/WAIT sources |

Core programming model above still applies 1:1 for I2S/SPI/UART-class work on RP2350.

---

## Mini encoding crib (debug / SM_INSTR)

```
JMP:  000  ddddd  Ccc  aaaaa     Ccc=condition, a=addr
WAIT: 001  ddddd  p  ss  iiiii   p=polarity ss=source
IN:   010  ddddd  sss  bbbbb     b=bitcount (0=32)
OUT:  011  ddddd  ddd  bbbbb
PUSH: 100  ddddd  0  if  0  blk  0 0000
PULL: 100  ddddd  1  ie  0  blk  0 0000
MOV:  101  ddddd  ddd  op  sss
IRQ:  110  ddddd  0 c w  iiiii
SET:  111  ddddd  ddd  iiiii
```
`ddddd` = delay/side field (layout depends on SIDESET_COUNT).

---

## One-screen I2S master skeleton

```
.program i2s_duplex
.side_set 1 opt
; sideset=BCLK  set=LRCLK  out=DOUT  in=DIN
; 8 clk/bit · autopull+autopush@32 · MSB first

    set x, 30
    set y, 1
    set pins, 0 side 0
.wrap_target
bit:
    out pins, 1 side 0 [3]
    in  pins, 1 side 1 [2]
    jmp x-- bit
    jmp y-- right side 0
left:
    set pins, 0
    set y, 1
    out pins, 1 side 0
    in  pins, 1 side 1 [1]
    set x, 30 side 1
    jmp bit
right:
    set pins, 1
    out pins, 1 side 0 [1]
    in  pins, 1 side 1 [2]
    set x, 30 side 1
.wrap
```

---

*Sources: RP2040 datasheet §3 (PIO), RP2350 datasheet PIO chapter, pico-sdk `hardware_pio` / pioasm.*  
*Saved for HT-15 / RP2350 work alongside `references/i2s-master-timing.md`.*
